summary:
Insulin treatment for gestational diabetes does not guarantee preventing congenital defects due to variable patient response based on genetics; different forms of MODY highlight the need for personalized intervention strategies beyond a one-size-fits-all approach relying solely on insulin therapy.
The effectiveness of insulin in treating maternal diabetes during pregnancy varies significantly depending on each woman’s specific genetic makeup, which can affect her body's ability to use insulin effectively—thus impacting fetal health differently. Different subtypes of maturity-onset diabetes of the youth further emphasize the necessity for customized therapeutic plans instead of uniform reliance on insulin as an absolute solution against potential birth complications associated with poorly managed blood sugar levels prenatally.
